proposed_catalog_row.Paper: The Performance of Forecast-Based Monetary Policy Rules under Model Uncertainty
proposed_catalog_row.KeyFeatures: Forecast-based Taylor rules; FRB-US cross-model evaluation; interest-rate smoothing; output-gap response
proposed_catalog_row.RawPDF: raw/mmb_papers/The Performance of Forecast-Based Monetary Policy Rules under Model Uncertainty.pdf

proposed_status_row.paper_title: The Performance of Forecast-Based Monetary Policy Rules under Model Uncertainty
proposed_status_row.raw_pdf_path: raw/mmb_papers/The Performance of Forecast-Based Monetary Policy Rules under Model Uncertainty.pdf
proposed_status_row.formula_quality: policy-rule formulas extracted; full FRB-US structural equations not printed in paper
proposed_status_row.steady_state_quality: linearized baseline described; full implementation steady state deferred
proposed_status_row.notes: Index author field lists John B. Taylor, but the paper title page lists John C. Williams; shared CSVs were not edited by instruction.

commands:
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re
model = 'US_FRB03'
base = Path('mmb-paper-derivations/derivations') / model
en = (base / f'{model}_derivation.en.md').read_text()
zh = (base / f'{model}_derivation.zh.md').read_text()
assert all(f'## {i}.' in en for i in range(1, 9))
assert re.search(r'\(F1\)', en)
assert len(re.findall(r'\(F\d+\)', en)) == len(re.findall(r'\(F\d+\)', zh))
print('archive_entry_checks_ok')
print('f_number_counts_match')
PY
results: English derivation has all eight required sections
English and Chinese F-number counts match

unresolved_issues: Full FRB-US structural equations are not printed in the LWW (2003) paper-side Markdown.
Detailed FRB-US derivation requires FRB-US documentation, not just the MMB .mod implementation.
Model-index author metadata should be reviewed in a separate shared-index task.
Runtime validation was explicitly out of scope.
